title: Anthropic study finds domain expertise still matters in agentic coding
friendlyTitle: Anthropic says knowing the problem still matters with coding agents
summary: Anthropic analyzed roughly 400,000 Claude Code sessions from about 235,000 users and found a division of labor: people make most planning decisions, while Claude makes most execution decisions. The study argues that domain expertise often matters more than formal coding background when using coding agents effectively.
friendlySummary: Anthropic studied Claude Code use and found that humans usually decide what should be built, while the agent handles many details of how to build it.
whyItMatters: This is useful guidance for anyone adopting coding agents: the human advantage shifts toward knowing the problem, constraints, and quality bar. Non-developers may become more productive, but expert judgment still shapes the result.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This means AI can help more people make software, but good results still depend on human knowledge and judgment.
